Lumber Posting Screen Help
This help window is for information only.  To navigate through the database you must first close this window.  To do this click on the blue oblong in the Status Area that contains the word “continue”

The Lumber Posting Screen is primarily used to enter time worked from cabinetmaker timesheets on Monday mornings for the previous weeks work.  Every cabinetmaker fills out a time sheet, on the back side is space to list lumber used for specific jobs.
These are the brown buttons in the top right corner of the Labor Posting screen

Create New
Record

Show all
Records
These are the Fields in this layout, starting from the top left, progressing to the left then down.
This button allows you to create a new record by opening a blank Labor Posting Input Screen.  You also have the option of using the key stroke “”Control” “N””.

This button allows you to show all of the records in the labor posting table.  This table is a group of records that represents all of the hours each cabinetmaker, or other individual within the company has worked on a specific job.
Job Number
Job Name
Item
Craftsman
Date of Use
In this field you will type in the number of the job you will be recording the lumber usage for.  Once you have entered the job number you will tab to the next field.
The information in this field is automatically filled in by the system, based on the job number.  When you tab after entering the job number this field will be skipped.
The information in this field is automatically filled in by the system, based on the job number.  When you tab after entering the job number this field will be skipped.

This is the next field you will enter information into.  This field is a drop down menu.  Click on the person’s name you are entering the information for.  The system will automatically take you to the next field for input once you click on your choice.

This date, if you are entering lumber information from timesheets, should be the week ending date for those timesheets.  Make sure you enter the same date for all the timesheets.  The format you should use for all dates is as follows:  MM/DD/YY.

This field is a drop down menu.  Click on the species of lumber the cabinetmaker has indicated on their timesheet.  The system will automatically take you to the next field for input once you click on your choice.
Type

The information in this field is automatically filled in by the system.  For this screen the “Type” is always “Lum” This field will be skipped, you will not be able to change the contents of this field.
On the backside of each cabinetmakers timesheet are spaces to fill in for lumber usage.  If someone has used some lumber they will first enter how many boards they used.  That number is placed here.

The information in this field is automatically filled in by the system. Based on your lumber species selection, the system takes specific information from the wood listing and determines what is the thickness of the lumber you are recording.

The information in this field is automatically filled in by the system.  This field is computed by the system based the following formula: 

Width X LengthX 12, the sum of which is then divided by the Thickness.

This allows us to see what the total board foot usage of this species, for this job is.
Progressing across the page the width of the board, in inches, will be recorded next.  That number is placed here.
Progressing across the page the Length of the board, in feet, will be recorded next.  That number is placed here. From here the rest of the information is automatically filled in by the system

NOTE:  If you notice repetitive listings for the; same job,same species of lumber, same thickness of lumber and same length of lumber, you can add up the widths for those entries and record them as one line item.  The length is NOT added up.

Cost per
Board Footage
Lumber Cost

The information in this field is automatically filled in by the system.  This is based on the Lumber Species that is listed.  This cost per board foot is representative of the cost at the time the information is recorded.

The information in this field is automatically filled in by the system. This is the cost at the time the original information was entered.   This figure is formulated from the following computation: 

Board Footage x Cost per Board Foot.

Lumber Species
Today

Cost per
Board Footage
Today

Lumber Cost
Today
The system automatically puts the same information in this field as you indicated in the “Lumber Species” field.

The information in this field is automatically filled in by the system.  This is based on the Lumber Species Today that is listed.  This cost per board foot today is representative of the cost at today’s prices for the lumber species.

The information in this field is automatically filled in by the system. This is the cost for the lumber at today’s prices. This figure is formulated from the following computation: 

Board Footage x Cost per Board Foot Today.

Once you have entered all of the information for a record, you may progress;

1 - To a new blank record by; keyboard strokes “Control N” or using the button “Create New Record”
2 - To a new layout or menu by; going to the status area and accessing the oblong under the “Layout” heading; by depressing the blue arrow and clicking on your choice of layout you wish to go to next
3 - Hide the system from view so you can work on something else; on the main navagation bar at the top of your screen you will see the words “Filemaker Pro”, if you click on this word a drop down menu of actions will appear.  Click on the entry “hide Filemaker Pro” and the screen will disappear into your dock area.
